title: [BUG] Security Vulnerability in "uuid" transitive dependency in exceljs latest version 4.4.0
author: max-chen-innosys
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

There is a new security vulnerability in the "uuid" transitive dependency used by exceljs's latest version 4.4.0.

Please refer to this link for reference: [https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq](url)

We discovered this by running the "npm audit" command which shows this in our terminal:

uuid  <14.0.0
Severity: moderate
uuid: Missing buffer bounds check in v3/v5/v6 when buf is provided - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq

and then subsequently "npm ls uuid" which shows this:

└─┬ exceljs@4.4.0
  └── uuid@8.3.2

Please advise if there are any plans to fix this vulnerability in exceljs, or how we can handle it. 

Thank you.

comments.author: gameroman
comments.body:
Try one of these which are more actively maintained forks

https://github.com/excel-js/exceljs

https://github.com/cjnoname/excelts

https://github.com/protobi/exceljs

comments.author: hellmanhellman
comments.body:
Add this to your package.json to override the uuid of your transitive dependencies (including exceljs) and make them use uuid >=14. Note that this will affect all the dependencies that use uuid, but it should be fine. I've confirmed it works for exceljs. 


```json
  "resolutions": {
    "uuid": ">=14.0.0"
  }
```

(be careful about trying random forks of packages, people can put anything they want in them and it's a common way to inject malware).

comments.author: CleberL
Ben-CA
brillant007
comments.body: After uuid upgrade to 14.0.0, there is a build issue in my project. Dose anyone have any advice?

comments.author: gameroman
comments.body: > After uuid upgrade to 14.0.0, there is a build issue in my project. Dose anyone have any advice? > > What Node version do you have? `uuid@14` is ESM only so it won't work if a package imports it using `require(...)` if you are on an old Node version Node 20 is EOL, so I recommend Node 22+

comments.author: Shuunen
comments.body: On my side I added an override "uuid": ">=11.1.1" and it work well 👍 The current version resolved/used is uuid 14 and my exceljs script still works as expected.
